title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oily face

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander tinctures helps with oily face because it has natural astringent and antiseptic properties that help to reduce excess sebum production and purify the skin. It also possesses antibacterial compounds that combat acne-causing bacteria, allowing for clearer and more balanced skin. Additionally, germander's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and congestion associated with oily complexions. As a result, regular use of herbal germander tinctures can help to achieve a healthier, more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Germander's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, leading to a reduction in sebum production and improvement in acne-prone skin.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Germander's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, while also preventing further damage caused by environmental factors.

Antimicrobial activity: Germander's antimicrobial properties can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne and oily skin, reducing the growth of this bacteria and subsequent inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinol: This diterpene has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria that may contribute to oily skin and acne.

Rosmarinic acid: As a phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.

Carvacrol: This monoterpene has been found to have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce the presence of bacteria and other microorganisms that can contribute to oily skin and acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids, which can help to reduce inflammation and have antioxidant properties that may help to balance the skin.
Stems: They also contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of oily skin.
Flowers: They are used in herbal remedies for their astringent properties, which can help to tighten pores and reduce the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves in late summer or early fall when in full bloom for 2 to 3 weeks.
Chop 1 part teucrium chamaedrys leaves with 2 parts apple cider vinegar in a clean glass jar to create  a mixture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally with a lid on.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks before using, label and date the jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with oily face because its antiseptic and astringent properties help to dry out pimples and reduce excess oil production. The herb's natural antibacterial compounds also combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing breakouts from forming in the first place. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties soothe redness and irritation, leaving skin looking calm and balanced. Regular use of sage tincture can help regulate sebum production, leading to a more even-toned and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative stress, leading to reduced sebum production and a balanced complexion.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in sage tincture help tighten pores, reduce oil secretion, and dry up acne-causing bacteria, resulting in a more even skin tone and reduced shine.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory agents reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, rosacea, and other skin irritations, promoting a clearer and healthier-looking complexion.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic diterpenoid, helps to reduce sebum production and inflammation in the skin, thereby controlling oily face.
Salvianolic acid A: A water-soluble phenolic acid, has potent antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing oiliness and preventing acne.
Ursolic acid: A terpenoid, has anti-androgenic properties that help to reduce sebum production and prevent oil buildup on the skin, leading to a clearer complexion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their astringent and antiseptic properties to help control oil production and reduce acne.
Flowers: Utilized for their ability to balance the skin's pH and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate oily skin issues.
Stems: Employed for their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties to help regulate the skin's natural oil production and prevent bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Clean the harvested material by gently brushing off dirt and debris from the plant parts.
Use a 1:5 ratio of plant material to solvent and soak 250ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar with the prepared plant material for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to separate the plant material from the tincture.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it correctly then store it in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel tinctures helps with oily face because of its astringent and antiseptic properties. The herb's natural ability to shrink pores and reduce sebum production makes it an effective remedy for controlling oiliness and acne-prone skin. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of witch hazel help to combat bacterial growth that can exacerbate oily skin. By balancing the skin's natural pH levels and reducing inflammation, American witch hazel tinctures provide a gentle yet powerful way to regulate oily skin and promote a healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel tincture helps to reduce oil production and tighten pores, making it an effective natural remedy for controlling acne and minimizing shine.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Witch Hazel tincture soothe and calm redness and irritation caused by oily skin, reducing the appearance of blemishes and acne scars.

Antioxidant properties: Witch Hazel tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to premature aging and discoloration, while also protecting against environmental stressors that can exacerbate oily skin.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Hamamelis virginiana tinctures help to reduce sebum production and tighten the skin's pores, minimizing the appearance of oily skin.

Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin is a unique tannin found in Hamamelis virginiana that has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to balance the skin's pH and reduce oil production.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Hamamelis virginiana tinctures have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.

Buds: Employed for their ability to balance the skin's pH, reduce oil production, and prevent bacterial growth that can cause acne.
Stems: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and twigs from a clean area by hand or pruning shears.
Dry the hamamelis virginiana material in a single layer at room temperature for 1 to 2 weeks.
Weigh and combine 1 part dried hamamelis virginiana with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain and discard the solids then transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for better quality.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean and well-ventilated area for at least 7-10 days.
Chop the dried plant material into smaller pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Steep 1 part of the dried plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place before straining and bottling.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Clean the leaves thoroughly by rinsing them with distilled water and gently pat them dry with a clean cloth.
Combine the leaves with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar in a ratio of 1:3.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh geranium maculatum leaves and flowers in the late spring or early summer.
Dry the harvested plant material at room temperature for 7-10 days or use a food dehydrator.
Use a glass jar with a lid to combine 1 part dried plant material with 2 parts 80-proof vodka.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh or 100 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in a clean bucket.
Chop the leaves and flowers into smaller pieces and combine them with 750 milliliters of vodka in the bucket.
Seal the bucket and steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks or 6 to 8 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean container and discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place and use it within 2 years after straining.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the taraxacum officinale plant roots and leaves on a sunny day when the dew has dried.
Clean the harvested plant parts by gently rinsing them with water for about 5 minutes.
Dry the cleaned plant parts in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Use a 1:2 ratio of the dried plant material to 95 percent ethanol to create the tincture mixture.
Steep the tincture mixture in a dark glass container for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the active ingredients to infuse.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with oily face because they contain powerful antioxidants that help to regulate the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves taking care not to damage the plant.
Blend 500g of harvested aloe vera gel with 250ml of vodka and 50ml of glycerin.
Transfer the mixture to a glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding solids.
Dilute the resulting tincture with distilled water to create a 1:3 ratio of aloe vera to water.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning or evening when the essential oils are most concentrated.
Weigh and chop 500 grams of harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves into small pieces for optimal extraction.
Combine the chopped leaves with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete leaf coverage.
Allow the mixture to steep at room temperature for eight hours or overnight, allowing for efficient solvent extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and preserving the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Corn tinctures helps with oily face because they contain potent herbs that deeply cleanse and purify the skin. The antimicrobial properties of these herbs, such as tea tree oil and lavender, combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to excess oil production. Additionally, corn tinctures can help regulate the skin's natural pH balance, reducing inflammation and congestion that can lead to oily skin. By balancing the skin's ecosystem, corn tinctures promote a healthy, radiant complexion with reduced oiliness.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Herbal corn tinctures containing ingredients like tea tree oil, lavender oil, or chamomile have antibacterial and antifungal properties that help to combat acne-causing bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts.

Astringent and Tonic Properties: Tinctures with astringent herbs like witch hazel, yarrow, or sage help to constrict pores, reduce sebum production, and balance the skin's natural pH, which can help to control oiliness and shrink the appearance of pores.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory herbs like calendula, comfrey, or St. John's Wort can help to soothe and calm red, inflamed skin, reducing irritation and acne lesions, while also promoting healthy skin regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in regulating sebum production and soothing acne-prone skin.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of large pores on an oily face.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh corn cobs of zea mays and rinse them thoroughly with clean water.
Chop the corn cobs into small pieces and combine with 50% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to steep the herbs.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years using 20-30 drops in facial toner morning and night.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with oily face because of its unique ability to balance and regulate the skin's natural oil production. The herb's astringent properties help to constrict pores, reducing their size and minimizing sebum output. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation often associated with oily skin. remedy_properties: Mucilage: Marshmallow tincture contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with oily skin.
Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to skin damage and premature aging, which can exacerbate oily skin issues.

Soothing astringent effects: The tannins present in marshmallow tincture have astringent properties that help to tighten pores and reduce excess oil production, making it an effective natural remedy for controlling oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily faces.
Barks: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne and other inflammatory skin conditions common with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried marshmallow root powder from the althaea officinalis plant.
Mix 100 grams of powdered marshmallow root with 500 milliliters of organic vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks to allow for a slow and steady extraction process.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding any solids remaining in the filter.
Label the tincture with ingredients dosages and preparation time instructions and store it in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture's ability to regulate sebum production reduces excess oil secretion, while its soothing qualities calm redness and irritation. Additionally, the tincture's antibacterial properties help to control acne-causing bacteria, preventing breakouts and blackheads. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in wild potato tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative stress, which can lead to oily skin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild potato tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with acne and other skin conditions that can exacerbate oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, roots help to absorb excess oil and purify the skin, reducing acne and blemishes.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, rhyzomes soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of taraxacum kok-saghyz and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine 100 grams with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily for two minutes each morning.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding the solids after one hour.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with oily face because it possesses antiseptic and astringent properties that help to reduce the production of sebum, thereby controlling oiliness. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers in full sun for maximum potency and oil-absorbing properties.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 90°f for 12 hours to preserve quality.
Combine one part dried hypericum perforatum with two parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean bottle, discarding solids, and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent properties, which help to balance the skin's oil production. remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered seeds with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily for at least 30 seconds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store them in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with oily face because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming acne-prone skin.
Flowers: The delicate flowers of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and promote a balanced skin pH, making it ideal for oily face care.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in full bloom for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by gently blowing off any excess moisture and storing them in an airtight container.
Mix 250 grams of dried flowers with 500 milliliters of vodka or ethanol-based solvent in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction, before straining the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Filter the resulting tincture into dark glass bottles using a fine-mesh sieve and store it in a cool, dark place.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oily face are liquid extracts of herbs used to help balance and regulate skin's natural oil production. They work by reducing inflammation, soothing pores, and tightening skin, resulting in a healthier and more even complexion. The best herbal tinctures for oily face include Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Lavandula angustifolia, Saponaria officinalis, Hamamelis virginiana, Rosmarinus officinalis, Geranium maculatum, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, oily skin can cause acne, clogged pores, and low self-esteem, negatively impacting daily life and affecting one's confidence and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily face that are treated with herbal tinctures are acne, eczema, and psoriasis. These conditions involve skin inflammation, excess oil production, or dryness, which can be effectively managed with herbal remedies. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like tea tree oil, lavender, and aloe vera have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, itching, and irritation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with oily face is a blend of Tea Tree (Melaleuca alternifolia) and Calendula (Calendula officinalis). Tea Tree tincture has antimicrobial properties that reduce acne and inflammation, while Calendula tincture helps to balance the skin's natural moisture levels and reduce sebum production. Adding a few drops of Geranium (Pelargonium graveolens) tincture can also help to regulate hormonal imbalances that may be contributing to oily skin. Use 5-7 drops of each tincture and mix with a carrier oil for a natural and effective solution.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with oily face because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which work to balance the skin's natural oil production. The tincture's astringent properties help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oiliness and preventing breakouts. Additionally, Calendula's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin helps to regulate the skin's natural pH, leading to a more balanced and healthier complexion. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to any skincare routine.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help with oily face by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of sebum, thereby controlling acne and preventing clogged pores.
Phenolic acids: These constituents help with oily face by possessing antioxidant properties that reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to acne and skin breakouts.

Flavonoids: These compounds help with oily face by their ability to reduce inflammation, improve blood circulation, and have antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria that can cause acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce acne and soothe the skin.
Leaves: They are utilized for their astringent properties, which help to tighten pores and reduce oil production in the skin.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to help regulate the skin's natural balance and reduce excessive oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers and clean them thoroughly to remove any debris.
Combine the cleaned flowers with 80-proof vodka at a 1:5 ratio and stir well to mix.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and let it steep for 2-3 weeks to extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.
Label and date the bottle and let it mature for 2-3 months before using the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with oily face because of its natural astringent and antibacterial properties. The tannins present in aloe vera help to reduce sebum production and tighten pores, thereby minimizing the appearance of oiliness. Additionally, aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin. By balancing the skin's natural pH and reducing bacterial growth, aloe vera tinctures can help to regulate oil production and achieve a healthier, more balanced complexion.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative helps reduce sebum production and inflammation in the skin, thus controlling oily skin.
Aloin: This anthraquinone glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the appearance of oily skin.

Aloe glycosides: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce the production of sebum, leading to a healthier and more balanced skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part in making tinctures due to their high concentration of aloe vera gel and other beneficial compounds.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds contain a high amount of aloin, a compound that helps to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce oiliness.
Leaves: Aloe vera leaves also contain a high amount of aloe-emodin, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat acne, making it beneficial for oily faces.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100% organic aloe vera leaves with thick fleshy parts for tincture preparation.
Wash the harvested aloe vera leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and add 50% of the total volume of 80-proof vodka.
Allow the chopped aloe vera and vodka mixture to steep for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with oily face because of its potent antimicrobial and antifungal properties. remedy_parts: Leaves: They are high in antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help control acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Buds: They contain essential oils with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against acne and skin infections.
Stems: They are rich in antimicrobial compounds that help balance the skin's pH and reduce the appearance of pores, making them suitable for oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves from healthy plants in the early morning when moisture levels are lowest.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Combine the cleaned leaves with 500ml of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with oily face because it contains compounds that help regulate skin's natural oil production, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritation. Clean and dry the harvested leaves for 24 hours to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Chop the dried aloe barbadensis leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine 1 part chopped aloe barbadensis with 2 parts 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for extraction and saturation.

Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place, use within 2 years.
